name: product-brief-and-app-scaffold
goal: Create the initial Next.js + TypeScript app scaffold for a pass-comparison website, define the product brief in code-adjacent docs/config, and prepare the repo structure for a Vercel deployment.
criteria: A Next.js + TypeScript application is scaffolded and runs locally.
The repo includes the core app structure for pages, components, and data files.
A concise product brief captures the target user, default NYC region behavior, supported common regions, and the app's decision-support goal.
The project is prepared for Vercel deployment with a clean install and build path.
progress: Scaffolded Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind via create-next-app
Created app/components/, app/data/, lib/ directory structure
Wrote lib/types.ts with typed schema stubs for Resort, Region, PassRecommendation
Replaced default page.tsx with pass-comparison landing copy (NYC default framing)
Updated layout.tsx metadata (title/description) to match app purpose
Created docs/product-brief.md covering target user, regions, heuristics, Vercel deployment notes
Verified clean npm install and production build (next build passes, static export at /)
Audit (codex exec review --uncommitted) run; stale metadata fixed per findings
notes: Default stack assumption: Next.js on Vercel with TypeScript. The app should not be a neutral spreadsheet; it should guide users toward Ikon Base or Ikon Pass using explicit heuristics and copy. NYC is the default region, but common alternates should be available via a region selector.

name: ikon-access-data-model-and-ingest
goal: Create the canonical local dataset for Ikon Base vs Ikon Pass access using official sources, including resort availability, access limitations, and blackout-date information.
criteria: A typed schema exists for resorts, pass access, blackout dates, and regional metadata.
Official Ikon source pages are identified and recorded in the codebase or logs for traceability.
The dataset clearly distinguishes full access, limited access, excluded resorts, and blackout-date applicability for each pass.
Validation catches malformed or incomplete data before the app consumes it.
progress: Fetched 2025-26 blackout dates from palisadestahoe.com, mammothmountain.com, bigskyresort.com — confirmed Dec 27-31 2025, Jan 17-18 2026, Feb 14-15 2026
Identified 6 base-excluded resorts (Alta, Aspen, Deer Valley, Jackson Hole, Snowbasin, Sun Valley) and 4 Bonus Mountains (full-only, 2-day)
Updated lib/types.ts: ResortAccess, BlackoutWindow, BlackoutSchedule types; removed stale top-level baseDays/hasBlackouts fields
Created app/data/resorts.ts: 59 resorts across all access tiers for 2025-26
Created app/data/blackoutDates.ts: BlackoutSchedule data with getBasePassBlackouts() helper
Created lib/validate.ts: validateResorts(), validateBlackoutSchedules(), validateAll()
Created docs/ikon-sources.md: full source traceability document with update guidance
Build passes (next build), TypeScript no errors (tsc --noEmit)
notes: Do not rely on memory for resort lists or blackout dates. Use current official Ikon sources and normalize them into app-owned structured data. The data model should be durable enough to support future season updates.

name: region-modeling-and-user-value-signals
goal: Model common user home regions and attach practical value signals so the site can rank which resorts matter most for each region instead of presenting an undifferentiated master list.
criteria: NYC is implemented as the default region with a defensible nearby/destination resort framing.
Additional common regions are supported with documented assumptions.
Each region has enough metadata to power useful sorting or highlighting, such as likely drive-to resorts versus flight destinations.
The app can surface region-aware value signals that feed into pass recommendations.
progress: Enhanced lib/types.ts: Region now has driveToTags, destinationResortIds, fullPassTriggers, nearestDriveHours, framing; added RegionValueSignals interface
Created app/data/regions.ts: 7 regions (nyc default, boston, chicago, denver, la, seattle, bay-area) with documented assumptions
Created lib/regionUtils.ts: getDriveToResorts, getDestinationResorts, getFullPassTriggerResorts, getRegionValueSignals, sortResortsByRegionRelevance helpers
Updated lib/validate.ts: validateRegions() and updated validateAll() signature to include regions
Build passes (next build), TypeScript clean (tsc --noEmit), codex exec review --uncommitted: no actionable findings
notes: This task is where product judgment matters. For NYC users, the recommendation should emphasize realistic East Coast usage patterns and note when the full pass only becomes worthwhile because of specific destination priorities or blackout sensitivity.

name: recommendation-logic-and-decision-framework
goal: Implement opinionated recommendation logic that tells users when Ikon Base is probably enough and when Ikon Pass is worth paying more for, based on resort access, blackout tolerance, and region-aware usage patterns.
criteria: The app exposes a clear recommendation framework rather than only raw tables.
Recommendation logic accounts for blackout dates, excluded resorts, and meaningful region-specific resort value.
Users can see the rationale behind a recommendation in plain language.
The logic is structured so that future tuning can happen without rewriting the UI.
progress: Created lib/recommend.ts: RecommendationInputs, RecommendationFactor, DetailedRecommendation types
Implemented getRecommendation(inputs, resortMap): pure function with 3 full-pass signals (excluded resort, blackout conflict, region trigger) and base-sufficiency baseline
Implemented getDefaultRecommendation(region, resortMap): region-only mode for initial page state
Scoring model: strong=10, moderate=3, weak=1; FULL_THRESHOLD=9 (one strong or two moderate flips to full)
Rationale builder: surfaces full-factor details in strength order; base case uses region framing
TypeScript clean (tsc --noEmit), build passes (next build), codex exec review: no actionable findings
notes: Bias toward useful advice. A likely baseline heuristic: recommend Ikon Base unless the user strongly values blackout-free peak dates, specific full-pass-only resorts, or a travel pattern that materially benefits from the full pass.

name: comparison-ui-and-explanatory-content
goal: Build the user-facing experience for comparing Ikon Base and Ikon Pass, including resort lists, blackout-date visibility, filters, and concise explanatory content that makes the recommendation understandable.
criteria: The site presents a direct Ikon Base vs Ikon Pass comparison above the fold.
Users can browse resorts by pass access and see blackout-date implications.
Region selection changes the framing or prioritization of resorts and recommendations.
The UI works well on desktop and mobile and avoids generic placeholder presentation.
progress: Created app/components/PassComparison.tsx: client component with region selector, blackout-sensitive toggle, recommendation card, resort table with checkboxes, access filter tabs, and search input
Updated app/page.tsx: server component that imports resorts/regions/defaultRegion and passes to PassComparison
Fixed scoring bug in lib/recommend.ts: FULL_THRESHOLD lowered from 9 to 5 so two moderate factors (blackout + region trigger) correctly flip to full pass recommendation

Fixed summary copy: corrected base-pass access description to '5 days at most resorts' and '~8 unlimited no-blackout' resorts; corrected full-pass bullets to '7-day access' for Alta/Aspen/Deer Valley/Jackson Hole/Snowbasin/Sun Valley
Build passes (next build), TypeScript clean (tsc --noEmit), codex exec review: all P1/P2 findings resolved
notes: The UI should lead with the decision, then justify it. Resort tables are supporting evidence, not the whole product. Keep the content concise and specific rather than marketing-heavy.

name: github-sync-and-vercel-launch
goal: Initialize Git if needed, connect the codebase to GitHub, deploy the site to Vercel, and verify the production build.
criteria: The project is a Git repository with coherent commits for the completed work.
A GitHub remote is configured and the codebase is pushed successfully.
The project is deployed on Vercel with a successful production build.
Deployment details and any required environment or operational notes are documented.
progress: This is user's comments: you should use vercel mcp. vercel cli constantly asks me (the human) to authorize. It's annoying
Created GitHub repo charliezchen/snowboard, pushed all commits to main branch
Verified Vercel production deployment (dpl_Apn3ZnqiQVgmiH3PQvHwS3emyoT5) is READY at snowboard-mu.vercel.app
Build logs confirm: Next.js 16.2.2 Turbopack, compiled in 4.2s, TypeScript clean
Created docs/deployment.md with GitHub, Vercel, and operational notes
notes: This task depends on having a destination GitHub repository or a way to create one. Keep deployment simple and static-friendly where possible.

name: resort-map-visualization
goal: Add a free-to-run resort map so users can understand geographic clustering, drive-to vs destination patterns, and which mountains matter for their selected region.
criteria: The resort data model includes stable latitude/longitude fields for every mapped resort.
The UI includes a map view that renders resort markers and stays in sync with region selection, search, and access filters.
Marker styling or popups make Base-included, Full-only, blackout-sensitive, and selected resorts easy to distinguish.
The implementation uses a free map stack with correct attribution and an explicit low-cost hosting posture.
progress: Added lat/lng fields to Resort type in lib/types.ts
Added coordinates (WGS 84) to all 59 resorts in app/data/resorts.ts
Added lat/lng validation to lib/validate.ts
Installed leaflet, react-leaflet, @types/leaflet
Created app/components/ResortMap.tsx: dynamic-import Leaflet map with color-coded CircleMarkers, popups with access details, add-to-list button
Integrated map into PassComparison between recommendation card and resort table, synced with region/filter/search state
Added legend below map for marker color meaning
Fixed orchestrate.sh hardcoded task name (codex audit P1)
Fixed map filtering to show all filtered resorts including international (codex audit P1)
Build passes (next build), TypeScript clean (tsc --noEmit)
notes: Start by extending the Resort type and app/data/resorts.ts records with lat/lng coordinates; the current dataset is not geo-enabled, so map rendering should not begin until coordinates are added and validated. Prefer a simple client-side marker map rather than a heavy GIS feature set. The default implementation path should be Leaflet plus react-leaflet because it is free, mature, and sufficient for a resort marker UI. The map should sit above or alongside the existing resort table and reuse the current filtering state so users do not see two disconnected browsing systems. Marker colors or icons should distinguish Base-included resorts, Full-pass-only resorts, blackout-sensitive resorts, and resorts the user has explicitly selected. Popups should show at least resort name, state/province, and Base vs Full access summary. For tile/data sourcing, do not treat the default OpenStreetMap community tile server as an unlimited production CDN; use proper attribution and keep usage modest. For a small hobby-scale deployment, OpenStreetMap tiles are acceptable if the implementation follows the tile usage policy and includes attribution. If the site is expected to get more meaningful public traffic, prefer a still-free stack with MapLibre and a free vector-tile source instead of assuming continued reliance on the default OSM tile server. Keep the code structured so the tile provider can be swapped later without rewriting the UI.
